title: Top 8 Herbal Tinctures for Natural Relief from Inflamed Gums

Alkylamides are good for inflamed gums because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is often used to help soothe inflamed gums. This is because Echinacea purpurea contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. When you apply Echinacea purpurea tincture to your inflamed gums, the active ingredients are absorbed quickly into the affected area. They then start to calm down the inflammation, which helps to ease the pain and discomfort. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture may also have antibacterial properties, which can help to prevent infections that can make gum inflammation worse. Using Echinacea purpurea tincture regularly may also help to reduce the frequency and severity of gum inflammation episodes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with inflamed gums, you will need dried licorice root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a clean glass measuring cup, vodka or a similar solvent, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. First, measure one part of dried licorice root and two parts of the solvent, such as vodka, into the glass jar. Stir the mixture well to ensure the licorice root is completely covered by the solvent. Next, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This will allow the active ingredients from the licorice root to extract into the solvent. After the two weeks, strain the mixture through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and keeping the liquid. Transfer the liquid tincture to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, mix a few drops of the tincture with water and swish it in your mouth before spitting it out, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The tincture made from this herb is good for inflamed gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When the tincture is applied to the gums, it helps to reduce swelling and ease pain. This is due to the presence of compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These compounds work by blocking the production of inflammatory chemicals and improving blood flow to the affected area. As a result, the inflamed gums begin to heal, and the pain subsides. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of Salvia officinalis help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for the gums to recover.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with inflamed gums, you'll need dried licorice root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like glycerin or vodka, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Start by measuring out one part of dried licorice root and adding it to the glass jar. Then, pour over it two parts of your chosen carrier oil, making sure the licorice root is completely covered. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the flavors mix. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to the dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, simply drop a few drops of the tincture onto your gums and massage it in, or mix it with water to create a mouthwash. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including inflamed gums. The tincture is made from the leaves of the Melaleuca tree and contains compounds like eucalyptol and cineole, which have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When applied to inflamed gums, the tincture helps to reduce swelling and kill bacteria that can cause infection. The antibacterial properties of the tincture work to combat the growth of bacteria that can lead to gum disease, while the anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm the gums, reducing pain and discomfort. Regular use of the tincture can also help to prevent further inflammation and promote healthy gum growth, leading to a reduction in inflammation and a healthier, more comfortable mouth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture for inflamed gums, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, start by harvesting or purchasing dried roots of the licorice plant (Glycyrrhiza glabra), and chop them into small pieces. Next, place the chopped roots in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid, and cover them completely with a neutral-tasting spirit like vodka or rum. Make sure the roots are fully submerged in the liquid by adding more spirit if needed. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the roots release their active compounds into the spirit. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Your tincture is now ready to use, and you can take 20-30 drops under the tongue as needed to help soothe inflamed gums.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture for inflamed gums, you will need the dried root of the licorice plant, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. First, measure out about 100 grams of the dried licorice root and place it in the glass jar. Next, pour enough solvent over the root to completely cover it, usually around 500 milliliters. Make sure the jar is well sealed and label it with the date and ingredients. Now, let the mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the root's active compounds to infuse into the solvent. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. remedies.remedy_desc: Commiphora molmol tincture is a herbal remedy that may help with inflamed gums. The tincture is made from the resin of the Commiphora molmol tree, which is native to Africa and the Middle East. This resin contains compounds with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tincture can be applied topically to the affected area, allowing the active compounds to penetrate and soothe the inflamed tissue. By reducing inflammation, Commiphora molmol tincture may help alleviate symptoms of gum inflammation, such as redness, bleeding, and discomfort. It may also help prevent the progression of gum disease by reducing bacterial growth and promoting a healthy gum environment. Some people use Commiphora molmol tincture as a natural alternative to traditional treatments for gum inflammation, and it is often combined with other herbal remedies for enhanced effectiveness.

Begin by measuring one part of the dried licorice root to two parts of the carrier oil in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is completely dry before adding the ingredients to avoid contamination. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture for inflamed gums, start by gathering high-quality roots of the licorice plant, Glycyrrhiza glabra. Clean and dry the roots thoroughly to prevent contamination. Next, use a clean glass jar with a lid to make the tincture. Fill the jar about one-quarter full with the dried licorice roots. Then, cover the roots with a neutral spirit like vodka or glycerin, making sure the roots are completely submerged. The general ratio is one part roots to two parts spirit, so for example, use 250ml of roots and 500ml of vodka. Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for at least 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the spirit extract the medicinal properties from the roots. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting tincture can be taken as needed, typically by mixing a few drops with water or another liquid, to help soothe inflamed gums and promote oral health.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture that helps with inflamed gums, start by gathering dried roots of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid, a sharp knife, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Cut the dried roots into small pieces and place them in the glass jar, filling it about 3/4 full. Next, pour the solvent over the roots, making sure they are completely covered. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration, and it allows the active compounds in the licorice roots to dissolve into the solvent. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is now ready to use. To take it, simply add a few drops to a glass of water or another liquid, and swallow it. The tincture can help soothe inflamed gums and reduce discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is good for inflamed gums because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and redness in the gums. The tincture is made from the roots of the licorice plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to soothe mouth and gum problems. The active compound in licorice root, called glycyrrhizin, has been shown to be effective in reducing inflammation and pain. When applied to the gums, the tincture can help calm irritated tissue and promote healing. Additionally, it may also help to reduce gum sensitivity and discomfort, making it easier to eat and speak. The tincture can be used in combination with other oral care practices, such as regular brushing and flossing, to help manage inflamed gums and promote good oral health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture for inflamed gums, first you need to dry and clean the roots of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. You can do this by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper towel or a clean cloth and letting them air dry for 2-3 days. Once the roots are dry, chop them into small pieces and fill a clean glass jar with the dried licorice root. Next, you will need to make a solvent, which is typically a high-proof vodka or rum, to extract the medicinal properties from the licorice root. Pour enough solvent over the licorice root to completely cover it and then seal the jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place, shaking it gently every day for 2-3 weeks to allow the solvent to extract the medicinal properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tincture, which can be used to help soothe and calm inflamed gums. You can take a few drops of the tincture in water or apply it directly to the affected area with a cotton swab, but it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a helpful remedy for inflamed gums. Echinacea purpurea, also known as purple coneflower, contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. It also has antimicrobial properties, making it effective against bacteria that can cause infections.  Salvia officinalis, or common sage, has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that can help soothe and protect the gums. Its antimicrobial properties can also help prevent further infections.  Inflamed gums can be painful and uncomfortable, and if left untreated, can lead to more serious issues such as tooth loss or heart problems. Gum disease has been linked to an increased risk of heart disease, so it's essential to address the issue promptly. When gum inflammation is treated, it can improve your overall health and reduce the risk of other health problems.  By using herbal tinctures, you can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in your gums. This can be especially helpful for people who are sensitive to antibiotics or prefer a more natural approach to healthcare. Regular use of herbal tinctures can also help to prevent gum disease from recurring, giving you peace of mind and confidence in your oral health.
supplementary_best: For inflamed gums, a combination of tinctures from Melaleuca alternifolia and Calendula officinalis is recommended. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antibacterial properties that help reduce inflammation and fight off infections that cause gum inflammation. Calendula officinalis, or marigold, is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm inflamed tissues.  The combination of these two tinctures may also benefit from the addition of Aloe barbadensis, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm irritated tissues. However, it's essential to note that Aloe barbadensis can be too harsh if used in high concentrations, so it's best to use a small amount.  Glycyrrhiza glabra, or licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed tissues, but it may interact with other medications and has side effects like high blood pressure. Therefore, it's recommended to use it with caution.  Other herbs like Echinacea purpurea, Salvia officinalis, Commiphora molmol, and Eucalyptus globulus, while beneficial for other health issues, may not be as effective in treating inflamed gums.
supplementary_avoid: If you have inflamed gums, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that may make the problem worse. One such herb is 'Ginkgo biloba', which can increase bleeding. 'Yarrow' (Achillea millefolium) and 'Willow bark' (Salix alba) may also be problematic due to their blood-thinning properties. 'Goldenseal' (Hydrastis canadensis) contains a compound called berberine, which can cause the gums to become more inflamed. 'Turmeric' (Curcuma longa) may be irritating to the gums and cause further inflammation, especially if taken in high doses. 'Clove' (Syzygium aromaticum) oil, which is sometimes used in herbal tinctures, can be too strong and cause mouth irritation or even damage the gums.
supplementary_other_remedies: Aloe vera gel is a natural remedy that can help soothe inflamed gums. Its anti-inflammatory properties can reduce swelling and ease pain. Aloe vera contains compounds called glycoproteins and polysaccharides, which can help calm the gums and promote healing. You can apply aloe vera gel directly to your gums using a clean cotton swab or toothbrush, or mix it with water to create a mouthwash. Aloe vera is gentle on gums and can be used in combination with other natural remedies.  Apple cider vinegar has also been used to help treat inflamed gums. It contains acetic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infection. Mix apple cider vinegar with water to create a mouthwash, and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute before spitting it out. However, be careful not to swallow the mixture, as it can be acidic and potentially harm your teeth.  Salt water rinse is another natural remedy that can help reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth. Dissolve a teaspoon of salt in a glass of warm water and swish it around your mouth for 30 seconds to 1 minute before spitting it out. The salt helps reduce swelling and kills bacteria, while the warm water helps loosen food particles and debris.  Cold compress can also be used to help reduce inflammation in the gums. Wrap an ice pack or a cold, wet cloth around your cheek and hold it there for a few minutes. The cold temperature can help reduce swelling and ease pain.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with inflamed gums are more likely to experience other health issues. For example, they may have bad breath (halitosis), which is caused by bacteria in the mouth. Medicinal herbs like neem and clove oil can help heal inflamed gums and reduce bad breath.  Those with inflamed gums are also at risk for tooth loss and abscesses. An abscess is a painful pocket of pus that can form in the gum. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and fight infection. It can also help with tooth loss by promoting healthy gum growth.  Inflammated gums can also increase the risk of heart disease. The bacteria that cause gum inflammation can enter the bloodstream and cause inflammation in the heart. A medicinal herb called ginkgo biloba has been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. This can help reduce the risk of heart disease.  In addition, people with inflamed gums may experience digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or acid reflux. This is because the bacteria that cause gum inflammation can also enter the digestive system and cause problems. Medicinal herbs like aloe vera and licorice root can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation.  Finally, people with inflamed gums may be more susceptible to diabetes. Research has shown that there is a link between gum inflammation and diabetes. Medicinal herbs like cinnamon and berberine have been shown to help reduce inflammation and improve insulin sensitivity, which can help manage diabetes.
